verdict_reasoning: ITPR3 is a multi-pass transmembrane ER-resident calcium channel with canonical localization to the endoplasmic reticulum and mitochondria-associated membranes (MAMs). Its transmembrane architecture (6 TM domains) and N-terminal cytoplasmic domain are entirely intracellular-facing. No documented plasma membrane pool, no ectodomain shedding, no covalent surface tethering, no cancer-state ecto-presentation, no cell-death externalization, and no activation-induced surface translocation. The protein is not a target of cell-surface-directed therapeutics.
